result.description: A container for other Sound generators.

parameters.description: The output volume as normalised linear gain (0.0 to 1.0), not decibels. Use a SimpleGain effect in the FX chain for decibel-scaled volume control.
The stereo balance
The maximum number of voices
The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer

modulation.description: Modulates the output volume
Modulates the pitch of all voices

midi.description: The main scripting interface for MIDI processing, UI creation, and plugin control via the HiseScript API.

fx.description: A parametric equalizer with unlimited filter bands and an FFT spectrum display for visual feedback.
Algorithmic reverb based on Freeverb with controls for room size, damping, and stereo width

parameters.description: Perceived room size from 0 (small) to 1 (large cathedral)
High frequency absorption where higher values create a darker reverb tail
Wet/dry mix where 0 is fully dry and 1 is fully wet
Level of the dry signal mixed into the output
Stereo width of the reverb where 0 is mono and 1 is full stereo
Freeze mode that sustains the reverb tail indefinitely

fx.description: Utility gain processor with optional delay, stereo width, and balance control, useful for level automation, simple timing offsets, and mid-side shaping

parameters.description: Output gain in decibels, where 0 dB is unity and negative values attenuate the signal
Static delay time in milliseconds applied to both channels
Stereo width percentage where 0 is mono, 100 is unchanged, and higher values exaggerate the sides
Stereo pan position of the output signal
Inverts the signal polarity for phase correction or mid-side processing

modulation.description: Modulates the output gain
Modulates the delay time
Modulates the stereo width
Modulates the stereo pan position

fx.description: Algorithmic reverb based on Freeverb with controls for room size, damping, and stereo width

parameters.description: Perceived room size from 0 (small) to 1 (large cathedral)
High frequency absorption where higher values create a darker reverb tail
Wet/dry mix where 0 is fully dry and 1 is fully wet
Level of the dry signal mixed into the output

children.description: A disk-streaming sampler with sample maps, round robin, crossfade groups, and timestretching.

parameters.description: The output volume as normalised linear gain (0.0 to 1.0), not decibels. Use a SimpleGain effect in the FX chain for decibel-scaled volume control.
The stereo balance
The maximum number of voices
The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer
Preload buffer size in samples for all loaded samples. Set to -1 to load the entire sample into memory.
Streaming buffer size in samples. Two buffers per voice are swapped between disk reading and audio output.
The actual number of allocated voices for sample playback
Number of round-robin groups. Also used as a general mapping dimension for velocity layers or articulations.
How the sampler handles retriggered notes on the same key
Transposes playback pitch based on the MIDI note relative to the sample root note. Disable for drum samples.
Plays the entire sample ignoring note-off events
Plays all round-robin groups simultaneously and crossfades between them using the Group Fade modulation chain
Memory management: Disabled keeps samples loaded, Enabled unloads all preload buffers, Lazy Load delays preloading until first trigger
Loads samples fully into the preload buffer and reverses the playback direction
Prevents the routing matrix from resizing when loading a sample map with a different mic position count
Filter order for the envelope follower low-pass filter, stored in multiples of 6 (0 = off, 6, 12, ...)

modulation.description: Modulates the output volume

children.description: An AHDSR envelope with adjustable curve shapes, optional downsampling for CPU savings, and per-voice modulation of all time parameters.

parameters.description: Enables monophonic mode where only one voice is rendered
Restarts the envelope when a new note is triggered in monophonic mode
Controls how long the envelope takes to reach full level after a note-on
The peak level reached at the end of the attack phase
The time the envelope stays at the attack level before decaying
The time the envelope takes to fall from the attack level to the sustain level
The level maintained while the note is held
The time the envelope takes to fall from the sustain level to zero after note-off
Controls the curvature of the attack phase (0.0 = concave, 1.0 = convex)
Controls the curvature of the decay and release phases
Enables 16x downsampling for reduced CPU usage

modulation.description: Modulates the attack time per voice
Modulates the attack level per voice
Modulates the decay time per voice
Modulates the sustain level per voice
Modulates the release time per voice
Modulates the pitch of all voices
Modulates the sample start position within each sample's SampleStartMod range. Voice-start-only, gain mode.
Crossfades between round-robin groups using the crossfade tables for dynamic layering

midi.description: Release trigger generator that replays notes on key-up with velocity scaled by a time-based attenuation curve

parameters.description: Enables time-based velocity attenuation on release
Time in seconds used to normalise the release attenuation curve
Curve that scales release velocity over time

children.description: A container for synthesisers that share common modulation, with optional FM synthesis and unison detune/spread.

parameters.description: The output volume as normalised linear gain (0.0 to 1.0), not decibels. Use a SimpleGain effect in the FX chain for decibel-scaled volume control.
The stereo balance
The maximum number of voices
The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer
Enables FM synthesis between two child synths
The child synth index used as FM carrier
The child synth index used as FM modulator
The number of unison voices per note
The detune spread in semitones for unison voices
The stereo spread of unison voices (0 = mono, 1 = full width)
Forces monophonic output by summing all voices
Kills retriggered voices instead of allowing multiple notes

children.description: Creates a modulation value from the MIDI velocity of incoming note messages, with optional table mapping and decibel conversion.

parameters.description: Inverts the velocity response so high velocities produce low values
Enables a lookup table for custom velocity response curves
Converts the output to a decibel scale (-100dB to 0dB) then to linear gain

children.description: Generates a periodic modulation signal with multiple waveform types, tempo sync, and an optional step sequencer mode.

parameters.description: The modulation frequency
Fade in time after each key press
The waveform for the oscillator
Disables retriggering of the LFO if multiple keys are pressed
Enables sync to host tempo
Smoothing time in milliseconds to reduce discontinuities in the modulation signal
Number of steps for the step sequencer
Enables oscillator looping
The initial phase offset of the LFO
Sync the LFO to the master clock
Free run mode - does not reset the LFO phase on note on

modulation.description: Modulates the intensity of the LFO output
Modulates the frequency of the LFO

fx.description: Stereo chorus effect with modulated delay lines for thickening and movement

parameters.description: LFO rate controlling the modulation speed
Modulation depth controlling the intensity of the pitch variation
Amount of signal fed back into the delay line for more pronounced sweeps
Base delay time that gets modulated by the LFO

children.description: A two-dimensional wavetable synthesiser that morphs between waveforms using a table index and supports audio file resynthesis.

parameters.description: The output volume as normalised linear gain (0.0 to 1.0), not decibels. Use a SimpleGain effect in the FX chain for decibel-scaled volume control.
The stereo balance
The maximum number of voices
The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer
Enables higher-quality rendering with better interpolation at the cost of more CPU usage
Index of the currently loaded wavetable bank from the project's wavetable directory
Normalized position in the wavetable where 0.0 is the first and 1.0 is the last waveform
Updates the mipmap when pitch modulation goes outside the frequency range to reduce aliasing

children.description: A lightweight sine wave generator for FM synthesis, additive synthesis, or adding subtle harmonics to other sounds.

The stereo balance
The maximum number of voices
The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer
Coarse pitch offset in octaves when using musical tuning mode
Fine pitch offset in semitones when using musical tuning mode
Switches between musical (octave/semitone) and harmonic ratio tuning modes
Harmonic index as a frequency multiplier where 1 is the root frequency
Fine frequency offset as a fractional multiplier in harmonic tuning mode
Amount of waveshaping saturation applied to the sine wave to add harmonics
